macro_architecture.reasoning: 2 active containers (backend, frontend) with 738 total components. Backend uses Layered pattern, Frontend uses Component-Based pattern.

container_analyses.summary: The backend implements a classic layered architecture built on Spring Boot, Spring MVC, Spring Data JPA, and Spring Security, exposing a comprehensive REST API for deed management, handover processing, and archiving. It delivers a rich set of domain capabilities across many entities and services, but the large number of controllers, services, and entity classes results in moderate coupling, potential service bloat, and technical debt that should be addressed to improve maintainability and scalability.

capabilities: Create, read, update and delete deed entries
Log and retrieve deed entry history
Manage actions and action streams
Handle document metadata storage and retrieval
Perform handover data set processing and versioning
Manage signature information and verification
Key management including re‑encryption and crypto state queries
Archiving operations such as token signing and archive status checks
Provide business purpose lookup
Manage connections between deed entries
Apply correction notes to deeds
Generate reports and job scheduling (including re‑encryption jobs)
Expose REST APIs for all above operations
functional.bounded_context: Deed Management, Handover & Archiving (Legal Document Registry)
integrations: External Key Management Service (KM) via REST
External Archiving Service for token signing
External Proxy/REST client services (configured via ProxyRestTemplateConfiguration)
Database persistence (JPA/Hibernate) as a data store

debt_indicators: Large number of controllers (32) – risk of fat controllers
Service layer has many implementations (42) – possible service bloat
High count of entity classes (199) – may indicate low domain aggregation
Direct DAO implementations used in services – missing higher‑level abstractions
recommendations: Refactor oversized controllers into smaller, request‑specific handlers
Apply Service‑Facade pattern to group related service methods and reduce service surface
Introduce domain aggregates to collapse related entity classes and simplify the model
Define repository abstractions or domain‑specific interfaces to decouple services from concrete DAO implementations
Adopt Interface Segregation on REST interfaces to avoid monolithic contracts
Consider consolidating similar services into bounded‑context modules to improve modularity

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The backend is functionally complete and follows a solid layered approach, but its size and lack of higher‑level abstractions create moderate coupling and technical debt. Targeted refactoring and domain modeling improvements will enhance maintainability and scalability.
top_recommendations: Refactor oversized controllers into smaller, request‑specific handlers
Introduce domain aggregates to collapse related entity classes and simplify the model
Define repository abstractions or domain‑specific interfaces to decouple services from concrete DAO implementations

container_analyses.summary: The frontend container is built on a component‑based Angular architecture (Presentation, Service, Adapter layers) leveraging TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Material and RESTful API integration to support the full spectrum of deed‑registration capabilities. While the implementation covers a rich set of domain concepts and integrations, quality metrics reveal moderate separation of concerns, coupling and cohesion, with a high count of services, adapters and pipes that inflate maintenance risk, resulting in an overall grade of C.

capabilities: Create, edit, view and delete deeds
Approve and correct deed entries
Manage deed workflow lifecycle (start, change, delete, reencryption)
Import and export documents (JSON, PDF, native formats)
Archive and retrieve document versions
Handle user authentication and authorization
Provide online help and notifications
Search and filter deed data (basic data, remarks, participants, connections)
Generate and export reports
Manage number assignments and sequencing
Execute background jobs and tasks
Interact with RESTful APIs for deed registry, document metadata, job and task services
Apply rate limiting, busy indicators and error handling for HTTP calls
functional.bounded_context: Deed Registration and Notarial Services Management

debt_indicators: Very large number of services (131) – likely some services are too big or overlapping
Excessive number of adapters (50) – possible duplication of data‑access logic
High count of pipes (67) – business logic may be misplaced in presentation layer
Potential mixing of domain, application, and UI concerns within modules
recommendations: Identify and split oversized services into smaller, focused ones or introduce facade layers
Consolidate similar adapters into a unified data‑access layer to reduce duplication
Move complex transformation logic from pipes into dedicated services
Review module boundaries; group related components, services, and adapters into feature‑specific modules
Introduce well‑defined interfaces between presentation and service layers to loosen coupling
Add documentation and tests for high‑traffic adapters to mitigate future maintenance risk

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The frontend provides comprehensive functional coverage and uses modern Angular tooling, but its architectural health is compromised by excessive service, adapter and pipe proliferation, leading to moderate cohesion and coupling. Refactoring to stricter modular boundaries and clearer responsibility allocation is needed to raise maintainability and scalability.
top_recommendations: Refactor oversized services into focused, purpose‑specific facades or smaller services.
Consolidate duplicate adapters into a unified data‑access layer with shared interfaces.
Extract heavy transformation logic from pipes into dedicated services to improve separation of concerns.

executive_summary: uvz is a modular monolith system with 738 components across 2 active containers: backend (333 components, Grade B), frontend (404 components, Grade C). Overall architecture grade: C.

Apply Service‑Facade pattern to group related service methods and reduce service surface
